sections.paragraph:
Ἑμμώρ, ὁ some edd.-όρ; indecl. (חֲמוֹר) (LXX.; JosAs 23:13.—Theodot. [II b.c.]: 732 Fgm. 1, 2 Jac [in Eus., PE 9, 22, 2] and Philo, Migr. Abr. 224 Ἑμώρ.—In Jos., Ant. 1, 337 Ἕμμωρος, ου) Hamor, from whose sons (TestLevi 5:4; 6:3; Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 9 Jac.), living near Shecḥem, Abraham bought a burial-place (Josh 24:32 Aq., Sym., cp. Gen 33:19; 34:2) Ac 7:16; BHHW II 692.

edd. edd. = editions, editors

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 323
